what: Use DSV3 officially-tuned 1x1 CK 2-stage kernels (64x32x32x128_1x1) for E=257 bs=16 and bs=128, replacing ksplit=2 cktile_moe. DSV3 CSV shows 1x1 kernel optimal for small tokens with E=257.
why: Current ksplit=2 overrides contradict official DSV3 tuning. The 1x1 (1-WG) kernel with 32x32 tiles is purpose-built for small per-expert GEMMs. ksplit=2 adds reduction overhead without enough CU underutilization to justify it (tg_num=288 >= 256 CUs for bs=16).

what: 4-WG stage1 + FlyDSL stage2 for bs=128/E=257/d=256 with block_m=32, replacing ksplit=2 cktile_moe
why: bs=512/E=257 improved with 4-WG+FlyDSL (v144). bs=128 has 1152 token-expert pairs which should benefit from 4-WG occupancy. v149 tried block_m=32 + FlyDSL but WITHOUT the 4-WG kernel. This combines both.

what: 4-WG stage1 M64 (256x64x128x128_1x4) for bs=512/E=33/d=2048 instead of M128, keeping FlyDSL t16x128x128 stage2
why: M128 block_m uses 128-row tiles for token grouping. With 512 tokens and 33 experts, avg ~14 tokens/expert. M64 tiles may give better CU utilization by having 2x more tiles to distribute across CUs. Original v037 used M64 for d=2048 showing -3.2%.

what: FlyDSL stage1 (flydsl_moe1_afp4_wfp4_bf16_t32x256x256) + FlyDSL stage2 for bs=128/E=33/d=512
why: FlyDSL stage1 for fp4 was previously blocked by server bug (b_scale kwarg). Server may have been updated. If FlyDSL stage1 works, it could replace CK stage1 for better performance by using unified FlyDSL pipeline.
override: Previous bug may be fixed in current server version

what: cktile_moe block_m=16 (instead of 32) for bs=16/E=33/d=512 with ksplit=2
why: Default cktile block_m heuristic uses 16 for token<2048. Current config overrides to 32 but smaller block_m may improve utilization for bs=16 (only 144 token-expert pairs). Fewer wasted slots in last tiles.

what_failed: Tried 5 variations: DSV3-tuned 1x1 CK kernels for E=257 (+9.5%), 4-WG+FlyDSL for bs=128/E=257 (+30%), M64 block for d=2048 (+1%), FlyDSL stage1 (blocked by dispatch), block_m=16 for bs=16/E=33 (+0.2%). All within noise or regression.
